message:
fix(core): always break bash RPC loop after kill; restrict session store truncation to last segment only

rpc.rs: when the abort signal fires during bash execution, guard.kill()
now always breaks the loop regardless of its return value. Previously,
Ok(None) or Err(_) from kill() would fall through, leaving the abort
handler stuck in the polling loop with a killed-but-not-reaped child
process. Now returns -1 for any non-success kill result.

session_store_v2.rs: the segment rebuild logic previously truncated ANY
segment file that contained corruption (oversized line, missing-newline
trailing frame, non-monotonic entry sequence) and then deleted all
subsequent segment files via remove_orphaned_segments(). This caused
silent data loss when an intermediate segment was corrupted — all later
segments (which may have been perfectly valid) were discarded.

Now:
- Corruption in the LAST segment still triggers truncation at the bad
  frame offset (safe: this is append-position recovery)
- Corruption in any INTERMEDIATE segment returns a hard error, forcing
  investigation rather than silently losing data
- remove_orphaned_segments() calls removed entirely — orphan cleanup
  should be an explicit maintenance operation, not a side effect of
  corruption recovery

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
